headline: Kirkwood Pulse • February 27, 2026
overview:
game_result: Cardinals 9, Astros 4
weather: Partly cloudy, 80 degrees
key_player_notes:
  -:
  player: Thomas Saggese
  note: Played outfield for first time since Little League to add defensive versatility; fielded one ball cleanly in left field; leaving camp Sunday to play infield for Italy in World Baseball Classic.
  -:
  player: Matthew Liberatore
  note: Pitched three innings, allowing one run on two hits and a walk with three strikeouts.
  -:
  player: Alec Burleson
  note: Hit a three-run home run, second consecutive game with a three-run homer.
  -:
  player: Yohel Pozo
  note: Hit a home run.
  -:
  player: Jimmy Crooks
  note: Hit a two-run home run.
  -:
  player: Jon Jon Gazdar
  note: Minor-leaguer who hit a home run and a double, scored go-ahead run in seventh inning.
  -:
  player: Bryan Torres
  note: Had two hits playing second base; leaving camp Sunday to play for Puerto Rico in WBC.
  -:
  player: Ivan Herrera
  note: Played first time this spring as DH, 0-for-2; scheduled to catch first time Sunday.
  -:
  player: Ramon Urias
  note: Expected to debut for Cardinals Friday, likely at third base.
  -:
  player: Zak Kent
  note: Designated for assignment to make room for Urias; claimed off waivers by Twins; third team since spring training started.
team_notes:
  -: Cardinals hit four home runs in the game.
  -: Starting leadoff hitters combined 0-for-12 in first six spring games.
  -: Quinn Mathews to start Friday against Mets; Tink Hence to make second relief appearance.
additional_info: Article by Rob Rains from Jupiter, Florida; follow on X @RobRains. plot: Matt Taylor, played by Kevin James, plans a dream wedding and honeymoon in Rome, but when things go awry and he can't get a refund, he embarks on the trip alone. Through this solo adventure, he experiences personal growth and new connections, discovering that heartbreak can lead to a better chapter in life.
review:
  tone: Lighthearted and sentimental
  style: A nostalgic throwback to classic romantic comedies of the 1960s, reminiscent of films like 'Come September' featuring picturesque Italian settings.
  performances: Kevin James delivers a relatable and sincere portrayal of a middle-aged man facing emotional loss. Supporting cast includes Julie Ann Emery, Alyson Hannigan, Kim Coates, Julee Cerda, Jonathan Roumie, and Nicole Grimaudo, who adds charm as a local café owner.
  direction: Chuck and Dan Kinnane use a familiar romantic comedy formula that is non-offensive, positive, and heartwarming.
  cinematography: Jared Fadel captures beautiful scenes of Rome and Tuscany, enhancing the romantic and travelogue feel.
  music: Features a soundtrack filled with familiar Italian songs.
  audience_recommendation: A feel-good movie ideal for a winter escape, offering warmth and lighthearted entertainment without profanity or violence. Rob Rains provides a one-minute video recap of the Cardinals' game on February 26, 2026, highlighting four home runs that contributed significantly to their win at Roger Dean Stadium. The video is available on the linked page, along with a podcast titled 'Rains on the Cardinals.'

items.title: Postcard from Cardinals spring training camp for Thursday, Feb. 26, 2026
items.excerpt: News and notes on the Cardinals' 9-4 win over the Astros on Thursday that include Thomas Saggese playing left field for the first time and four home runs, including Alec Burleson's three-run shot. [...]The post Postcard from Cardinals spring training…

items.title: Solo Mio
items.ai_summary:
plot: Matt Taylor, played by Kevin James, plans a dream wedding and honeymoon in Rome, but when things go awry and he can't get a refund, he embarks on the trip alone. Through this solo adventure, he experiences personal growth and new connections, discovering that heartbreak can lead to a better chapter in life.
review:
  tone: Lighthearted and sentimental
  style: A nostalgic throwback to classic romantic comedies of the 1960s, reminiscent of films like 'Come September' featuring picturesque Italian settings.
  performances: Kevin James delivers a relatable and sincere portrayal of a middle-aged man facing emotional loss. Supporting cast includes Julie Ann Emery, Alyson Hannigan, Kim Coates, Julee Cerda, Jonathan Roumie, and Nicole Grimaudo, who adds charm as a local café owner.
  direction: Chuck and Dan Kinnane use a familiar romantic comedy formula that is non-offensive, positive, and heartwarming.
  cinematography: Jared Fadel captures beautiful scenes of Rome and Tuscany, enhancing the romantic and travelogue feel.
  music: Features a soundtrack filled with familiar Italian songs.
  audience_recommendation: A feel-good movie ideal for a winter escape, offering warmth and lighthearted entertainment without profanity or violence.
